message:
feat(interactive): PERF-7 render pipeline optimizations — zero-copy cache hits, buffer reuse, and Cow-based viewport

Eliminate per-frame allocations in the TUI render hot path by threading
reusable buffers through the render pipeline and avoiding String clones
on cache hits.

src/interactive/perf.rs:
- Add append_cached() to MessageRenderCache: writes directly into caller's
  buffer on cache hit instead of returning a cloned String, eliminating
  one allocation per cached message per frame
- Add prefix_append_to(): zero-copy alternative to prefix_get() that
  appends the cached prefix directly into the output buffer
- Add header and footer fields to RenderBuffers with 512-byte initial
  capacity for chrome rendering
- Add INITIAL_CHROME_CAPACITY constant (512 bytes)

src/interactive/view.rs:
- Replace render_header()/render_footer() with render_header_into() and
  render_footer_into() that write directly into the output buffer,
  eliminating two intermediate String allocations per frame
- Replace owned conversation_content consumption with Cow<str> borrowing,
  ensuring the reusable conversation buffer is always returned to the pool
  regardless of which code path is taken (empty/welcome vs populated)
- Scope viewport slicing into a block to limit Cow lifetime

src/interactive.rs:
- Add prefix_cache_valid_for_test(), prefix_cache_len_for_test(), and
  render_buffer_capacity_hint_for_test() integration test helpers for
  PERF-2 and PERF-7 verification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
